heading: Permitting process improvement
label: 4370m–2 Permitting process improvement

children.heading: Project initiation and designation of participating agencies
children.label: a Project initiation and designation of participating agencies

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A project sponsor of a covered project shall submit to the Executive Director and the facilitating agency notice of the initiation of a proposed covered project.

children.heading: Default designation
children.label: B Default designation
children.text: If, at the time of submission of the notice under subparagraph (A), the Executive Director has not designated a facilitating agency under for the categories of projects noticed, the agency that receives the notice under subparagraph (A) shall be designated as the facilitating agency.section 4370m–1(c)(1)(B) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: a statement of the purposes and objectives of the proposed project;

children.label: ii
children.text: a concise description, including the general location of the proposed project and a summary of geospatial information, if available, illustrating the project area and the locations, if any, of environmental, cultural, and historic resources;

children.label: iii
children.text: a statement regarding the technical and financial ability of the project sponsor to construct the proposed project;

children.label: iv
children.text: a statement of any Federal financing, environmental reviews, and authorizations anticipated to be required to complete the proposed project; and

children.label: v
children.text: an assessment that the proposed project meets the definition of a covered project under and a statement of reasons supporting the assessment.section 4370m of this title

children.label: i
children.text: kept confidential; and

children.label: ii
children.text: exempt from the disclosure requirements under (commonly known as the “Freedom of Information Act”) and the Federal Advisory Committee Act (5 U.S.C. App.).section 552 of title 51See References in Text note below.1

children.label: i
children.text: identify all Federal and non-Federal agencies and governmental entities likely to have financing, environmental review, authorization, or other responsibilities with respect to the proposed project; and

children.label: ii
children.text: invite all Federal agencies identified under clause (i) to become a participating agency or a cooperating agency, as appropriate, in the environmental review and authorization management process described in .section 4370m–4 of this title

children.heading: Deadlines
children.label: B Deadlines
children.text: Each invitation made under subparagraph (A) shall include a 14 calendar day deadline for a response to be submitted to the facilitating or lead agency, as applicable.

children.label: i
children.text: has no jurisdiction or authority with respect to the proposed project; or

children.label: ii
children.text: does not intend to exercise authority related to, or submit comments on, the proposed project.

children.heading: Changed circumstances
children.label: B Changed circumstances
children.text: On request and a showing of changed circumstances, the Executive Director may designate an agency that has opted out under subparagraph (A)(ii) to be a participating or cooperating agency, as appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: give the participating agency authority or jurisdiction over the covered project; or

children.label: B
children.text: expand any jurisdiction or authority a cooperating agency may have over the proposed project.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: On establishment of the lead agency, the lead agency shall assume the responsibilities of the facilitating agency under this subchapter.

children.heading: Redesignation of facilitating agency
children.label: B Redesignation of facilitating agency
children.text: If the lead agency assumes the responsibilities of the facilitating agency under subparagraph (A), the facilitating agency may be designated as a cooperative or participating agency.

children.heading: Change of facilitating or lead agency
children.label: 6 Change of facilitating or lead agency

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: On the request of a participating agency or project sponsor, the Executive Director may designate a different agency as the facilitating or lead agency, as applicable, for a covered project, if the facilitating or lead agency or the Executive Director receives new information regarding the scope or nature of a covered project that indicates that the project should be placed in a different category under .section 4370m–1(c)(1)(B) of this title

children.heading: Resolution of dispute
children.label: B Resolution of dispute
children.text: The Chairman of the Council on Environmental Quality shall resolve any dispute over designation of a facilitating or lead agency for a particular covered project.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Executive Director, in coordination with the Administrator of General Services, shall maintain an online database to be known as the “Permitting Dashboard” to track the status of Federal environmental reviews and authorizations for any covered project in the inventory described in .section 4370m–1(c)(1)(A) of this title

children.heading: Specific and searchable entry
children.label: B Specific and searchable entry
children.text: The Dashboard shall include a specific and searchable entry for each covered project.

children.heading: Existing projects
children.label: i Existing projects
children.text: Not later than 14 days after the date on which the Executive Director adds a project to the inventory under , the Executive Director shall create a specific entry on the Dashboard for the covered project.section 4370m–1(c)(1)(A) of this title

children.heading: New projects
children.label: ii New projects
children.text: Not later than 14 days after the date on which the Executive Director receives a notice under subsection (a)(1), the Executive Director shall create a specific entry on the Dashboard for the covered project, unless the Executive Director, facilitating agency, or lead agency, as applicable, determines that the project is not a covered project.

children.heading: Projects other than covered projects
children.label: iii Projects other than covered projects

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The Executive Director may direct a lead agency to create a specific entry on the Dashboard for a project that is not a covered project and is under review by the lead agency if the Executive Director determines that a Dashboard entry for that project is in the interest of transparency.

children.label: aa
children.text: a comprehensive permitting timetable, as described in subsection (c)(2)(A);

children.label: bb
children.text: the status of the compliance of each lead agency, cooperating agency, and participating agency with the permitting timetable required under item (aa);

children.label: cc
children.text: any modifications of the permitting timetable required under item (aa), including an explanation as to why the permitting timetable was modified; and

children.label: dd
children.text: information about project-related public meetings, public hearings, and public comment periods, which shall be presented in English and the predominant language of the community or communities most affected by the project, as that information becomes available.

children.heading: Explanation
children.label: B Explanation
children.text: If the facilitating agency or lead agency, as applicable, determines that the project is not a covered project, the project sponsor may submit a further explanation as to why the project is a covered project not later than 14 days after the date of the determination under subparagraph (A).

children.label: i
children.text: make a final and conclusive determination as to whether the project is a covered project; and

children.label: ii
children.text: if the Executive Director determines that the project is a covered project, create a specific entry on the Dashboard for the covered project.

children.label: I
children.text: the notification submitted under subsection (a)(1);

children.label: aa
children.text: where practicable, the application and supporting documents, if applicable, that have been submitted by a project sponsor for any required environmental review or authorization; or

children.label: bb
children.text: a notice explaining how the public may obtain access to such documents;

children.label: III
children.text: a description of any Federal agency action taken or decision made that materially affects the status of a covered project;

children.label: IV
children.text: any significant document that supports the action or decision described in subclause (III);

children.label: V
children.text: information on the status of mitigation measures that were agreed to as part of the environmental review and permitting process, including whether and when the mitigation measures have been fully implemented; and

children.label: VI
children.text: a description of the status of any litigation to which the agency is a party that is directly related to the project, including, if practicable, any judicial document made available on an electronic docket maintained by a Federal, State, or local court;

children.label: ii
children.text: any document described in clause (i) that is not available by hyperlink on another website; and

children.label: iii
children.text: information about project-related public meetings, public hearings, and public comment periods, which shall be presented in English and the predominant language of the community or communities most affected by the project, as that information becomes available.

children.heading: Deadline
children.label: B Deadline
children.text: The information described in subparagraph (A) shall be posted to the website made available by hyperlink on the Dashboard not later than 5 business days after the date on which the Federal agency receives the information.

children.label: A
children.text: the permitting timetable established under subparagraph (A) or (C) of subsection (c)(2);

children.label: B
children.text: the status of the compliance of each agency with the permitting timetable;

children.label: C
children.text: any modifications of the permitting timetable;

children.label: D
children.text: an explanation of each modification described in subparagraph (C); and

children.label: E
children.text: any memorandum of understanding established under subsection (c)(3)(B).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 60 days after the date on which the Executive Director must make a specific entry for the project on the Dashboard under subsection (b)(2)(A), the facilitating or lead agency, as applicable, in consultation with each coordinating and participating agency, shall establish a concise plan for coordinating public and agency participation in, and completion of, any required Federal environmental review and authorization for the project.

children.label: i
children.text: A list of, and roles and responsibilities for, all entities with environmental review or authorization responsibility for the project.

children.label: ii
children.text: A permitting timetable, as described in paragraph (2), setting forth a comprehensive schedule of dates by which all environmental reviews and authorizations, and to the maximum extent practicable, State permits, reviews and approvals must be made.

children.label: iii
children.text: A discussion of potential avoidance, minimization, and mitigation strategies, if required by applicable law and known.

children.label: iv
children.text: Plans and a schedule for public and tribal outreach and coordination, to the extent required by applicable law.

children.heading: Memorandum of understanding
children.label: C Memorandum of understanding
children.text: The coordinated project plan described in subparagraph (A) may be incorporated into a memorandum of understanding.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: A Establishment
children.text: As part of the coordinated project plan under paragraph (1), the facilitating or lead agency, as applicable, in consultation with each cooperating and participating agency, the project sponsor, and any State in which the project is located, and, subject to subparagraph (C), with the concurrence of each cooperating agency, shall establish a permitting timetable that includes intermediate and final completion dates for action by each participating agency on any Federal environmental review or authorization required for the project.

children.label: i
children.text: the size and complexity of the covered project;

children.label: ii
children.text: the resources available to each participating agency;

children.label: iii
children.text: the regional or national economic significance of the project;

children.label: iv
children.text: the sensitivity of the natural or historic resources that may be affected by the project;

children.label: v
children.text: the financing plan for the project; and

children.label: vi
children.text: the extent to which similar projects in geographic proximity to the project were recently subject to environmental review or similar procedures under State law.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Executive Director, in consultation with appropriate agency CERPOs and the project sponsor, shall, as necessary, mediate any disputes regarding the permitting timetable referred to under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Disputes
children.label: ii Disputes
children.text: If a dispute remains unresolved 30 days after the date on which the dispute was submitted to the Executive Director, the Director of the Office of Management and Budget, in consultation with the Chairman of the Council on Environmental Quality, shall facilitate a resolution of the dispute and direct the agencies party to the dispute to resolve the dispute by the end of the 60-day period beginning on the date of submission of the dispute to the Executive Director.

children.label: I
children.text: be final and conclusive; and

children.label: II
children.text: not be subject to judicial review.

children.label: I
children.text: the facilitating or lead agency, as applicable, consults with the Executive Director regarding the potential modification not less than 15 days before engaging in the consultation under subclause (II);

children.label: II
children.text: the facilitating or lead agency, as applicable, and the affected cooperating agencies, after consultation with the participating agencies, the Executive Director, and the project sponsor, agree to a different completion date;

children.label: III
children.text: the facilitating agency or lead agency, as applicable, or the affected cooperating agency provides a written justification for the modification; and

children.label: IV
children.text: in the case of a modification that would necessitate an extension of a final completion date under a permitting timetable established under subparagraph (A) to a date more than 30 days after the final completion date originally established under subparagraph (A), the facilitating or lead agency submits a request to modify the permitting timetable to the Executive Director, who shall consult with the project sponsor and make a determination on the record, based on consideration of the relevant factors described under subparagraph (B), whether to grant the facilitating or lead agency, as applicable, authority to make such modification.

children.heading: Completion date
children.label: ii Completion date
children.text: A completion date in the permitting timetable may not be modified within 30 days of the completion date.

children.heading: Limitation on length of modifications
children.label: iii Limitation on length of modifications

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: Except as provided in subclause (II), the total length of all modifications to a permitting timetable authorized or made under this subparagraph, other than for reasons outside the control of Federal, State, local, or tribal governments, may not extend the permitting timetable for a period of time greater than half of the amount of time from the establishment of the permitting timetable under subparagraph (A) to the last final completion date originally established under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Additional extensions
children.label: II Additional extensions
children.text: The Director of the Office of Management and Budget, after consultation with the project sponsor, may permit the Executive Director to authorize additional extensions of a permitting timetable beyond the limit prescribed by subclause (I). In such a case, the Director of the Office of Management and Budget shall transmit, not later than 5 days after making a determination to permit an authorization of extension under this subclause, a report to Congress explaining why such modification is required. Such report shall explain to Congress with specificity why the original permitting timetable and the modifications authorized by the Executive Director failed to be adequate. The lead or facilitating agency, as applicable, shall transmit to Congress, the Director of the Office of Management and Budget, and the Executive Director a supplemental report on progress toward the final completion date each year thereafter, until the permit review is completed or the project sponsor withdraws its notice or application or other request to which this subchapter applies under .section 4370m–9 of this title

children.label: I
children.text: A determination by the Executive Director under clause (i)(III).

children.label: II
children.text: A determination under clause (iii)(II) by the Director of the Office of Management and Budget to permit the Executive Director to authorize extensions of a permitting timetable.

children.heading: Consistency with other time periods
children.label: E Consistency with other time periods
children.text: A permitting timetable established under subparagraph (A) shall be consistent with any other relevant time periods established under Federal law and shall not prevent any cooperating or participating agency from discharging any obligation under Federal law in connection with the project.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Each Federal agency shall conform to the intermediate and final completion dates set forth in the permitting timetable established under subparagraph (A), or with any intermediate or final completion date modified under subparagraph (D).

children.label: I
children.text: promptly submit to the Executive Director for publication on the Dashboard an explanation of the specific reasons for failing or reasonably believing the agency will fail to conform to the completion date and a proposal for an alternative completion date;

children.label: II
children.text: in consultation with the facilitating or lead agency, as applicable, establish an alternative completion date; and

children.label: III
children.text: each month thereafter until the agency has taken final action on the delayed authorization or review, submit to the Executive Director for posting on the Dashboard a status report describing any agency activity related to the project.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: If the facilitating or lead agency, as applicable, has a reasonable basis to doubt the continuing technical or financial ability of the project sponsor to construct the covered project, the facilitating or lead agency may request the project sponsor provide an updated statement regarding the ability of the project sponsor to complete the project.

children.heading: Failure to respond
children.label: ii Failure to respond
children.text: If the project sponsor fails to respond to a request described in clause (i) by the date that is 30 days after receiving the request, the lead or facilitating agency, as applicable, shall notify the Executive Director, who shall publish an appropriate notice on the Dashboard.

children.heading: Publication to Dashboard
children.label: iii Publication to Dashboard
children.text: On publication of a notice under clause (ii), the completion dates in the permitting timetable shall be tolled and agencies shall be relieved of the obligation to comply with subparagraph (F) until such time as the project sponsor submits to the facilitating or lead agency, as applicable, an updated statement regarding the technical and financial ability of the project sponsor to construct the project.

children.heading: Cooperating State, local, or tribal governments
children.label: 3 Cooperating State, local, or tribal governments
i
children.text: have jurisdiction over the covered project;

children.label: ii
children.text: are required to conduct or issue a review, analysis, opinion, or statement for the covered project; or

children.label: iii
children.text: are required to make a determination on issuing a permit, license, or other approval or decision for the covered project.

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: B Coordination
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable under applicable law, the facilitating or lead agency, as applicable, shall coordinate the Federal environmental review and authorization processes under this subsection with any State, local, or tribal agency responsible for conducting any separate review or authorization of the covered project to ensure timely and efficient completion of environmental reviews and authorizations.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Any coordination plan between the facilitating or lead agency, as applicable, and any State, local, or tribal agency shall, to the maximum extent practicable, be included in a memorandum of understanding.

children.heading: Submission to Executive Director
children.label: ii Submission to Executive Director
children.text: The facilitating or lead agency, as applicable, shall submit to the Executive Director each memorandum of understanding described in clause (i).

children.heading: Applicability
children.label: D Applicability
children.text: The requirements under this subchapter shall only apply to a State or an authorization issued by a State if the State has chosen to participate in the environmental review and authorization process pursuant to this paragraph.
the availability of information and tools, including pre-application toolkits, to facilitate early planning efforts;
key issues of concern to each agency and to the public; and
issues that must be addressed before an environmental review or authorization can be completed.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A lead agency may designate a participating agency as a cooperating agency in accordance with part 1501 of title 40, Code of Federal Regulations (or successor regulations).

children.heading: Effect on other designation
children.label: 2 Effect on other designation
children.text: The designation described in paragraph (1) shall not affect any designation under subsection (a)(3).

children.heading: Limitation on designation
children.label: 3 Limitation on designation
children.text: Any agency not designated as a participating agency under subsection (a)(3) shall not be designated as a cooperating agency under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Reporting status of other projects on Dashboard
children.label: f Reporting status of other projects on Dashboard
A
children.text: a total investment of more than $200,000,000; and

children.label: B
children.text: an environmental impact statement under NEPA.

children.heading: Effect of inclusion on Dashboard
children.label: 2 Effect of inclusion on Dashboard
children.text: Inclusion on the Dashboard of information regarding projects subject to or shall not subject those projects to any requirements of this subchapter.section 139 of title 23section 2348 of title 33
